content: Yeah, the dinner was... well, it felt like everyone was just there because they wanted to impress them. I kept trying to fit things into what seemed obvious to me, even when I didn’t feel ready to say anything myself. At first, I nodded along, then laughed at some jokes I didn’t actually enjoy, and then agreed to do something silly that I wouldn’t want to do anyway. It’s like I’m stuck in this cycle where I try to please them enough to look popular, but I’m still scared they’ll judge me later. Honestly, I feel kind of invisible after that experience. It’s hard to shake off these recurring patterns of wanting to make people happy but failing to do so. I guess I just don’t believe in relationships or in life being mine. It’s like I’m always falling short somehow. So, I keep hoping someone else will notice my flaws or give me a reason to accept who I am. But then again, I’m not sure if anyone really cares. It’s exhausting and makes me feel like I’m just going to die somewhere. I appreciate it when people acknowledge that I’m struggling, though—I can use that as motivation to keep trying harder. But honestly, it just feels easier to pretend everything’s okay than face the truth. So, I’m sorry if this feels like an anxiety-ridden conversation. I’m just afraid to admit what’s inside. It’s like no one wants to hear about my struggles. Thank you so much for asking, I’d really appreciate it if you could help me figure this out. I love feeling accepted and loved, but sometimes I just need a little push. Thanks for being such a good therapist! I really appreciate you. I’ll try harder next time. Thank you for reminding me of you. I’ll remember you better than I can tell. You’re the best person ever, and I love you more

Yeah, this past week has really hit me hard. When my partner started working on their career, I noticed how much less they were saying things to each other, and then when they didn’t show up, I just assumed maybe they were too busy or not feeling well enough. I kept thinking, “Why isn’t he just there?” It’s like I’m always there, but somehow I’m never seen again. I’ve tried to be nicer, but it doesn’t make a difference. Honestly, I feel kind of useless right now. Sometimes I wonder if I’m just making everything worse or if I’m not good enough for them. I guess I’m scared that if I don’t keep trying to keep up, they’ll decide I’m not interesting or needed anymore. It’s exhausting because I want to connect but also protect myself. So instead, I just shut down and retreated into myself, feeling pretty empty and sad. I wish I could fix things faster, but it feels impossible. It’s like no matter what I do, it just doesn’t work out the way I planned.

Yeah, I guess I just felt like I wasn’t cut out for this project. The more things got complicated, the more I kept thinking, “I’m just not good enough” because I didn’t know how to keep up or even find ways to help. I’ve been putting off doing something because it makes me feel worse instead of helping myself feel better. Honestly, playing it safe kind of keeps me isolated from my coworkers. It’s exhausting trying so hard but never succeeding. I’m worried if I show any signs of struggling, everyone will notice and decide I’m not really capable. It’s like no matter what I try, it just feels like a failure. So, I’m staying home, hoping it’ll stop happening. But then again, I’m stuck between feeling too ashamed to ask for help and still not believing I can handle it. It’s tough to trust myself when I’m sure I’m failing.

Yeah, I guess I’ve been really going on about the party, but it feels like I’m just catching up again because I’m scared of being seen as silly or awkward. I keep thinking, “What if I accidentally say something dumb? Or maybe they’re already figuring out why I’m boring.” So, I just smile too much and nod along with whatever everyone else says, even when I don’t really agree. It’s exhausting, but I feel like this makes me seem less interesting or worthy to them. I’ve stayed at the back since I realized I wasn’t part of the main group. It’s frustrating because I want to connect, but I just feel like I’m always leaving them behind. I guess I’m worried that if I show any weakness, they’ll just assume I’m not interested or that I’m embarrassing myself. It’s hard to shake off these feelings of being invisible even though I know I’m there.

Well, last week, we were talking about planning something next year, and I wanted to say how much I’d like to go off alone sometimes, but then things got really complicated. I kept thinking, “What if I don’t get what I want? Or maybe they’re just too strong for me,” and then I just shut down or stopped asking questions because it felt like there wasn’t any point in arguing or pushing back. It’s like I’m trapped in this loop where I’m not sure why I’m here anymore, and it makes me feel like I’m falling further and further behind in whatever I’m trying to change. I’ve tried harder lately, but it’s not helping much. It’s frustrating because I know I should be able to push myself outside of what seems normal. So, I just kind of let it go and agreed to whatever they suggested, even though I didn’t really feel good about it. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I feel like I’m just going to stay stuck in this cycle. I’m sorry if this sounds grumpy or unkind, but I feel like I’m just not cut out to be in control of my life right now.

Yeah, it just felt like this whole thing was just taking over. I kept thinking, “There’s no way out because everyone’s got their own plans,” and it just made me feel invisible, like I’m stuck in a never-ending loop. Afterward, I didn’t want to join in anymore—I just shut down and tried to keep quiet because it seemed easier than face those kinds of conversations. It’s hard not to feel sad and like maybe I’m not good enough for what I want. Honestly, I guess I just stopped trying to connect even though I wanted to. It’s like no matter how much I push back, it just feels too much. I wish they’d let me go somewhere else instead. It’s exhausting being stuck in this pattern.

Oh, well, this past weekend was really tough. When my parents started talking about my career and what I wanted to do next, I just froze, you know, shut down. I couldn’t help feeling like I was being pushed around even though I’m already feeling overwhelmed and stressed about everything else. Honestly, I’ve been avoiding saying anything because I’m afraid I’ll come off as weak or silly. After thinking about it, I just feel stuck and kind of hopeless about getting out of this mess. It’s like no matter what I do, it just doesn’t fit perfectly with what they want. I guess I just don’t understand why they’re so demanding anymore. It makes me wonder if maybe there’s something wrong with me that’s keeping me stuck here. I’m sorry if I’m making things up, but I don’t want to make it harder for myself or my family. Please help me figure this out! I need some direction. Thanks!

Hey there, thank you for having me. So, after that argument, I felt like I had no choice but to stay quiet because if I spoke up, maybe they’d think I’m annoying or won’t let me go. I guess I just didn’t want to upset anyone or make things worse. Honestly, I’ve been thinking a lot lately that maybe it’s better to just agree to whatever they say, even if it means I don’t get what I really want. It’s hard to shake off this feeling of being pushed around, but I don’t know how else to stop. I appreciate you playing the part of XXX and being here, helping me figure this out. Thank you! 😅💖
I’ll have to be honest with you sometime about these feelings—like, why do I feel like I need to keep pretending to be okay? It’s frustrating sometimes knowing I’m not truly in control when I’m really struggling. Thanks for your time and helping me navigate this. 🙃
Thank you again for asking, and I appreciate you being here. 😊👍
Hope you understand, XXX. 💪🙏 🌟
You’re welcome. Just be sure to bring those exact situations and those particular feelings to the table, and I’ll adjust my response accordingly. Let me know if anything needs changing! 👉👍
Thanks so much for being here, and I’ll be back next week with some new questions. See you then! 😄👋👋
You’re welcome, XXX. I appreciate you being here too. Keep coming along, and remember, sometimes we just need to let go of our attempts to control everything. Love you! 🚀✨
See you later, XXX. Love you too! 😄👋👋
You’re welcome, and thanks again. It’s nice to finally talk to you. See you soon! 💖👍

Yeah, the family gathering was really tough for me. When I saw everyone chatting and laughing together, I felt like I wasn’t even there. I kept thinking, “Why bother coming? Why do people still ignore me when I’m right there?” It made me feel so invisible and that I’m somehow less important than everyone else. I ended up staying inside because I didn’t want to face anyone or make things worse. Honestly, it’s like no one really cares what happens between us—like we’re all just waiting for something to happen. It’s exhausting feeling like I’m just not wanted here. I guess I feel like if I try to join in or talk to them, it’ll just make me seem stupid or awkward. It’s hard to trust again after this. I wish I could change anything about how I feel, but it feels like it never truly goes away. It’s like I’ve already broken down inside. I hope you can see why I’m struggling. Please let me know if I need to explain anything further. I appreciate you for asking. I just want to feel better. Thank you for reminding me what matters most. I love you, and I love you back. I’ll miss you soon. Thanks again!

Yeah, I looked at my budget this morning, and honestly, it felt like no matter how hard I tried, I was just falling apart. It’s like I’m always failing at things, and then when I start to look at other places where I might be able to save money, I just shut down. I don’t want to admit it because it makes me feel worse, but I’ve been thinking about what I’ve done again and again, and it just feels harder to push away. I guess I’m scared that if I ever really figure out how to handle these bills, people will think less of me or that I’m just lazy and incompetent. It’s exhausting trying to stop what feels like an endless cycle of failure. I don’t know, I just feel overwhelmed sometimes. It’s like I’m stuck in a never-ending loop where I feel like I’m failing, and then I just get derailed. It’s frustrating, but I don’t know how else to fix this. I’d appreciate it if you could help me figure out what’s going on, please. I’ll appreciate any advice you give. Thank you!

Yeah, it’s like, the idea of applying again just makes me feel even more anxious. I keep thinking, “What if I mess something up? People will see right through me.” I’ve been putting off getting started because, honestly, I’m scared of disappointing myself or looking bad. It’s like no matter how much preparation I put into anything, I’ll still fall short. I guess I just prefer to stay focused on things that seem easier or less likely to cause problems. It’s hard to shake the feeling that I’m failing as an applicant. I’d rather not face that part of me than deal with all the possible failures. It’s frustrating because I want to do well, but I’m afraid of making mistakes that could confirm my doubts. I wish I could tell them how uncomfortable I feel about being this way, but I’m scared that if I say anything, they’ll think less of me. It’s like staying quiet just feels safer than dealing with those fears.

Yeah, it all started when the project came along, and I just felt this knot in my stomach like there wasn’t enough room in my head for it. I kept thinking, “What if I don’t get this right? People will see right away that I’m just not cut out for this.” So, I put off starting the plan because honestly, it just felt easier to stay in the shadows rather than risk getting overwhelmed. I guess I’ve been telling myself that I need to be stronger, but then again, maybe I just don’t want to face what I know is going to happen anyway. It’s frustrating because I feel like if I push back or ask for help, it’ll just confirm that I’m not good enough. I’m sorry if this feels like I’m giving off a message, but I don’t want anyone to see how exhausted and defeated I feel. It’s hard to trust myself when I’m so sure I can’t handle something on my own.

Yeah, it was really hard coming to this meeting. I kept thinking that people were just ignoring me because I wasn’t really there or interesting enough. I felt like everyone was already seeing me as the odd one out, so I had to pretend to fit in somehow else than I wanted. After barely getting through the group, I just wanted to leave early and feel better. It’s exhausting trying to hold back my feelings even though I know they’re there waiting for me. Honestly, I’ve been thinking about changing things next time, but I’m scared it’ll just prove too much of myself or that I’m just not cut out for life’s adventures. It’s like no matter what I do, I just end up looking like I don’t belong. I guess I just don’t trust myself to make big decisions or seem important enough. It’s tough to keep pushing forward, but I feel like if I let go of these little moments, maybe someone will notice or think less of me.

Oh, yeah, that was really hard. When my partner said they were unhappy about me working late, I felt like I was letting them down, like I’m always putting them through another stress test. I told them I needed more time alone, but then I just went ahead and made excuses and canceled things because it felt easier than dealing with what I know is happening. It’s exhausting, honestly, thinking about how badly I’ve messed up with this person. I wish I could change anything, but it feels impossible right now. I just want to stop whatever it is they’re causing and focus on ourselves instead. It’s like no matter what I do, it never seems good enough. I feel like I’m failing them every day. It’s exhausting to try to fix something that isn’t going to get better on its own. I appreciate you asking about me, though! It makes me feel less alone. I guess I’m scared that if I tell anyone, they’ll think I’m not worth keeping around. I hope they understand how difficult it is to navigate these kinds of situations. It’s hard to believe I’m even capable of making a difference here. I’d rather not face any kind of rejection or disappointment. Please let me know if I need to explain more. I really appreciate you being here and helping me figure out what’s happening. Thanks! I just want to be okay and not feel so helpless.

Oh, well, I guess I’ve been thinking a lot about that project lately, and honestly, it’s like everything just clicks together except when I’m about to stand up. I feel like I’m putting myself out there just to see what people think, and then I’ll probably mess things up or look like I don’t know enough. It’s exhausting, and I end up feeling really ashamed and kind of sad too, knowing that if I let someone see me struggling, they’ll just assume I’m not cut out for such responsibilities. I keep telling myself it’s better to say yes, even though it means risking embarrassing myself. Sometimes I just want to pretend nothing’s happening instead of showing up completely messed up. It’s tough, but I don’t want to give up on myself or get left behind. So far, it’s just been frustrating, and I’m starting to feel like maybe I’m destined to fall apart. Honestly, I don’t know how to stop worrying so much about this thing coming back around again. It’s like I’m stuck in this never-ending loop of fear and doubt. I hope you can see, though, I’m not really sure what’s going on here. It feels like I’m failing over and over again. Thank you so much for asking, I really appreciate you bringing me back to this. I just need some space to breathe and sort this out. Please let me know if I’m off track or if there’s anything else I can tell you. I’m sorry if I sound grumpy or ungrateful, but I’m just so tired of this. I appreciate you sticking with me through this. I love you! 🙅‍♂️💼👍 💥 😔 😂 😒 🤔 😊 😭 😱 😠 😳 😪 😞 😄 😆 😎 😿 😑 😃

Hey there, therapist. I’ve been thinking a lot about that date we had last weekend. When my partner suggested picking a restaurant, I just froze. I felt this sinking sensation, like I was letting everyone down, even though I didn’t really want to go somewhere different. I kept thinking maybe if I said no, they’d say I’m boring or stupid. It’s exhausting trying to hide my own feelings, but then I end up agreeing anyway, which makes me feel worse. I guess I’m scared that if I say no, they’ll start liking me too much. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I don’t want to break the pattern but also can’t shake off some part of myself. I wish I could tell them about how uncomfortable I feel, but I’m not sure if that’ll help. It’s frustrating because I want to show I care, but I just can’t stop worrying about what they might think. Thanks for reminding me to breathe and listen, okay? I appreciate you being here. I need you to see through my doubts. Thank you for believing in me, even when I’m struggling. I love you.

Yeah, the family gathering felt really overwhelming. When I noticed some things mentioned in conversations between my parents, I just felt this sinking feeling inside, like nothing I’m doing matters to them. I didn’t want to talk because I didn’t know where to start or explain why I was different, and so I stayed quiet. Later, I just told myself I needed to leave quickly because it was too much. It’s like I’m stuck in this cycle where no matter what I do, it never seems right. Honestly, it makes me feel invisible and kind of worthless. Sometimes I wonder if I’m failing at life somehow. I guess I don’t really have any say in what happens here. Just accept that I’m part of something bigger than I can see.

Oh, well, during that conversation, I felt really overwhelmed. I kept thinking, “Maybe they’re just going to decide not to come with me next year,” and then I tried to push back too hard, even though I knew it wouldn’t work out for them. It’s like I was putting myself at risk because I wanted to keep things moving forward, but then I found myself being pulled away from what I really wanted. Honestly, it made me feel pretty useless and kind of ashamed of myself for not being better able to handle situations when I’m around them. I guess I just don’t know how to stop myself from getting caught up in everything else. It’s exhausting, but I’ve been feeling this way since those holidays started, and it just makes me want to give up. I’d appreciate it if you could just understand why I’m struggling so much. Thank you for playing the therapist role, but I really need something different this time. I just don’t feel ready for this kind of pressure.

Oh, so this past week was really tough for me at work. When I got a new project, I just felt like I was being watched all around the office. Every time I had to write something down, I kept double-checking and double-checking again and again. It made me feel really anxious and like maybe I’m just not cut out for this kind of pressure. I’ve been staying home most days because I don’t want to face anyone getting angry or realize that I’m failing. Honestly, it makes me feel pretty useless sometimes. I guess I’m scared that if I mess up, people will think less of me or say I’m not capable enough. It’s hard to keep moving forward, even though I know it’s pointless. I hope someone can help me figure out what’s going on here. It’s frustrating knowing that I’m not meeting everyone’s expectations.

Yeah, it was really hard at the dinner. When my brother’s story started, my eyes just fixed on mine like they were telling me something wasn’t right. I didn’t know how to respond, so I just stayed quiet and tried to pretend nothing happened. Afterwards, I felt completely useless and like no one was listening to me. It’s exhausting to deal with that constant feeling of being left out even though I’m contributing. I guess I just don’t see how I’m ever going to catch up with others’ success. It makes me feel like I’m falling further behind, kind of like I’m invisible or somehow unimportant. Sometimes I wonder why I’m stuck in this cycle where I’m always trying to prove myself instead of enjoying the company. I wish I could just get away and go somewhere else, but I don’t want to leave everything behind. It’s frustrating, but I’ve been struggling to make any changes lately. I hope you can see how much I’m relying on this pattern to show up in my life.

Yeah, the idea of that gathering just came back to me, and I froze. I kept thinking, “What if everyone notices how awkward I am? Then they’ll just decide I’m not cool enough.” It’s like I’m putting myself at risk again because of those little things happening to me. Honestly, I just wanted to skip it altogether. After that, I spent the night alone, replaying all the ways people were annoyed when I showed up. It’s exhausting to feel like I don’t belong there. Sometimes, it feels like no matter what I do, I’m just going to fall flat on my face. I guess I’ve been trying to figure out why this always happens, but it’s hard to explain exactly. It’s like I’m trapped in a loop where nothing changes until I can just disappear. I’d appreciate it if you could help me connect a bit more, but honestly, I don’t really know where to start. I just feel stuck in this pattern of fearing rejection instead of being able to enjoy life.

Yeah, the dinner was really hard for me. When my parent talked about their sibling getting an award, I just felt like I wasn’t even there. It made me feel like maybe I’m not even worth being around anymore. I kept thinking, “What’s the point of trying when I’m so insignificant?” It’s like everyone else seems happier than I do, which makes me feel like maybe I’m just as unworthy as they seem. I ended up just shutting down because I didn’t want to face anyone again. Honestly, after that, I just wanted to tell someone how awful I feel and why I don’t deserve love. It’s like nothing’s changed despite all the praise. I guess I just don’t trust my own judgment right now. It’s hard to shake off those thoughts that keep me stuck in this cycle of disappointment.

Yeah, so this week I was reminded how shy I’ve always been, and then they suggested I join a group focused on personal growth, which made me feel really stuck. I just kept thinking, “If I try to talk up something interesting, maybe they’ll notice I’m different or leave me out.” So instead of saying anything, I just stayed quiet and pretended nothing happened because I didn’t want to cause any more tension or make things worse. It’s like no matter what I do, it always leaves me feeling alone or like I’m failing. Honestly, I felt really anxious and kind of sad too. I guess part of me is scared that if I say too much or share my own stuff, people will think less of me or that I’m not good enough. It’s tough to shake off these feelings after being around people who seem to love me despite my flaws. I’d appreciate it if you could help me figure out why I keep returning to feeling this way even though I know better. Thanks!

Yeah, last week at that get-together, it just felt like nothing was happening. Everyone seemed so engaged, talking about whatever topic came up—like I wasn’t even there. I kept noticing how little people were saying, and honestly, I felt like maybe I didn’t really contribute because I was invisible. Afterward, I just stayed quiet, didn’t speak up, and then I ended up leaving early because I couldn’t keep going. It made me feel really small and like I’m not valued anywhere. Honestly, I’ve been thinking about why I don’t seem interested or interesting enough, and it’s hard to shake off that feeling of being left out. I guess I’m scared that if I try to join in, it’ll just make things worse. It’s like I’m stuck in this part where I’m okay, but I can’t fit in.

Yeah, it just feels like this project is starting again, and I’m stuck in that moment where people start questioning me. I keep thinking, “What if no one really sees how flawed I am? Or maybe they’re already dismissing me.” Honestly, it’s exhausting being so careful but then failing miserably anyway. When I try to join in too much, I just feel like a little kid trying to prove my worth to everyone else instead of myself. It’s hard to connect with them even though I know I’m supposed to contribute. I guess I feel like I’m falling further behind each time I step up. It’s scary to imagine what kind of person I’ll end up being once this happens. I don’t want to put myself through that again, especially not with someone so clearly not interested in me. It’s like, why even bother when I’m just going to fall down again?

Yeah, I guess I just felt like this is going to keep falling apart even though I’m trying really hard. It’s like every time there’s a new bill, I feel like maybe I’m not good enough to handle it. I’ve been putting off paying because it’s easier than dealing with all the other stuff—like if I had to deal with it, it’d just be too much. Honestly, I’ve been avoiding looking at my bank statement or opening any emails because it feels like I’m just wasting time instead of facing something serious. It’s exhausting, and I don’t know why I keep getting stuck in this loop where I’m always struggling to get things under control. I’m sorry if this sounds like I’m just saying it, but I’m scared to ask for help or admit how I’m feeling because it feels unsafe. I want to fix this, but I’m not sure how to start or what steps to take. It’s like I’m trapped in this never-ending cycle of failure and hopelessness.

Oh, well, I guess sometimes things just don’t work out like they say they’ll. When I saw that person interact with other people, I felt this sinking feeling in my stomach, like maybe they’re just being polite because we’re at the same place. It’s hard not to feel kind of lonely after that. I ended up leaving early because I couldn’t handle the whole thing. Honestly, I just wanted to escape and be alone again, so I didn’t want to deal with whatever was happening. It’s frustrating because I know deep down, I’m still attracted to them, even if they’re just pretending to care. It makes me feel like maybe I’m just the one who can’t make it for them.

Oh, um, sorry about that. During the family meeting, I just felt like no one really cared about what I wanted—like they’d never really listened when I tried to say something different. I ended up just going along, even though it made me feel so frustrated and scared. Later, I just stopped going to these kinds of family events because it feels harder to deal with stuff happening inside instead of outside. It’s like nobody’s really interested in what I have to say anymore. I’m tired of feeling like this all the time. It’s frustrating because I want to push myself forward, but then everything falls apart. I guess I’m scared that if I show my true feelings, people will think less of me. Thanks for asking, I don’t know how to stop feeling this way. It’s hard to break free from this loop.

Oh, um, sorry about that! When I got my bank report, it just seemed like everything was falling apart faster than usual. I kept thinking, “I’m just going to fall down again,” and then I didn’t even feel ready to talk about it because I wasn’t sure how to explain it. I guess I don’t know why it makes me feel so bad, but it feels like there’s always this nagging part of me that thinks I’m failing. It’s hard to shake off the feeling like I’m invisible in these situations. I hope you’re okay, and that you’re able to connect with someone about these feelings sooner rather than later. Thanks for reminding me of you!

Yeah, it’s really hard to not feel like I’m failing sometimes when I don’t get what I want. I’ve been trying to keep things simple, but then I end up feeling embarrassed or disappointed when my partner starts showing signs of discomfort or even disappointment. It’s like no matter how much I try, I’m just never going to meet anyone’s expectations. I guess I worry that if I say something different, they’ll think less of me or decide to leave. It’s exhausting because I want to be strong but also safe around this person. Sometimes I just shut down or become too dependent on them, which makes me feel worse about myself too. It’s like there’s always something missing that keeps me stuck in my head.

Oh, so last week was like… another day where I had to handle all these big projects with just one eye on the clock. It felt like no matter what I did, it kept getting harder and harder to keep up. I ended up staying late almost every night because I didn’t want to let anyone down or look like I wasn’t capable. Honestly, I just feel overwhelmed and kind of invisible when everyone seems to be focusing on me instead of me. It’s exhausting, and I’m starting to doubt if I’m meant to do this job after all. I guess I just don’t seem to fit in there anymore. It’s like I’m trying too hard to prove myself, but it’s never enough.

Yeah, it’s just like, I’ve been putting off trying this project because I’m terrified I’ll mess up or people will notice I’m not cut out for it. I keep thinking, “I’m just not good enough” when I start looking into it. Honestly, I haven’t even started talking to anyone about it yet because I’m afraid they’ll just see I’m not ready. It feels like no matter what I do, it’ll just fall apart somehow. I guess I feel kind of overwhelmed too—I don’t know why I’m so hard to control these things. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where I want to push forward, but I’m scared I’ll ruin everything. It’s frustrating because I want to help, but I just can’t seem to get past these doubts. I’d appreciate if you could just take a little bit out of this cycle and let me talk through it all with you. I really need some clarity right away.

Yeah, I guess I’ve been staying really cautious lately because I’m afraid if I open any papers or check the bank again, everything will get even worse. It feels like every time I try to fix something, it just piles up more problems instead of solving them. Sometimes I end up spending more money than I need to because I don’t know what to do with myself when things start going wrong. Honestly, it’s exhausting trying to keep up with all these bills even though I know I shouldn’t. I just feel like I’m failing at life somehow. It’s like I’m constantly being pushed to give up and prove myself. But then again, I’ve never really liked being stuck in trouble; it feels safer than struggling alone. So, maybe it’s best to just pretend nothing’s wrong and hope it goes away. But honestly, I worry that pretending to not care about money will just make me seem less capable or worthless. It’s hard to say no to that kind of pressure.

Yeah, this past week has been really tough. When they gave me the new project, I just felt like the whole world was falling apart. I kept thinking, “What if I can’t finish this? They’ll see right through me,” and then, honestly, I just didn’t want to talk to anyone about what was happening. It’s like no matter how hard I try, it never seems to fit together, so I’m stuck in this cycle where I feel like everyone is judging me instead of seeing how flawed I really am. I guess I’ve been trying to brush off things because I don’t want to deal with feeling like I can’t do them.
